# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }
truefix-session = { path = "crates/truefix-session" }
truefix-transport = { path = "crates/truefix-transport" }
truefix-config = { path = "crates/truefix-config" }
truefix-store = { path = "crates/truefix-store" }
truefix-log = { path = "crates/truefix-log" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.
opt-level = 3

# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J â€” first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: ðŸš§ Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** â€” stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** â€” the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** â€” protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields â€” they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI â”€â–¶ S1 Core codec â”€â–¶ S2 Minimal session â”€â–¶ S3 Recovery/resend â”€â–¶ S4 Data dictionary
   â”€â–¶ S5 Store/log â”€â–¶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect â”€â–¶ S7 All versions + codegen
   â”€â–¶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring â”€â–¶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator â†” acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) â†’ protocol correctness â†’ production-readiness â†’ feature count.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

# Migration Notes

Breaking-change and upgrade notes for TrueFix. Per Constitution Principle I, breaking changes to the
public API are recorded here with a semantic-version bump (FR-028).

## Unreleased â€” feature 002 (QuickFIX/J parity completion)

### Application callback signatures are now typed (breaking) â€” FR-016 / US5

The `Application` trait's fault-returning callbacks change from opaque `Result<(), String>` to typed
outcomes carrying a reason code and optional reference tag:

| Callback | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| `from_admin` | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), Reject>` (admin/logon refusal â†’ Logout + disconnect) |
| `to_app`     | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), DoNotSend>` (suppress outbound) |
| `from_app`   | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), BusinessReject>` (â†’ 35=j) |

`Ok(())` is unchanged. An `Err("...")` becomes the matching typed variant:

- `Reject { reason, ref_tag, text }` â€” returned from `from_admin`. The engine sends a Logout
  carrying `text` (if any) and disconnects. `reason`/`ref_tag` are not currently placed on the
  Logout (Logout has no SessionRejectReason field); they are reserved for callers who want to
  record structured context.
- `DoNotSend` â€” returned from `to_app`. The engine does not write the message to the wire and does
  not persist it to durable storage; the sequence number it consumed is gap-filled (not replayed)
  on a subsequent ResendRequest.
- `BusinessReject { reason, ref_tag, text }` â€” returned from `from_app`. The engine emits a
  Business Message Reject (35=j) carrying `reason` (BusinessRejectReason, tag 380), `ref_tag`
  (RefTagID, tag 371) if given, and `text` (tag 58). The inbound message's sequence number still
  advances normally.

These types are exported from `truefix_core` (re-exported by the `truefix` facade). Implementors
update their callback bodies to return the typed variants. No back-compat string variants are
retained.

**Example migration**:

```rust
// Before
async fn from_app(&self, msg: &Message, _id: &SessionId) -> Result<(), String> {
    if !self.known_symbol(msg) {
        return Err("unknown symbol".to_owned());
    }
    Ok(())
}

// After
async fn from_app(&self, msg: &Message, _id: &SessionId) -> Result<(), BusinessReject> {
    if !self.known_symbol(msg) {
        return Err(BusinessReject { reason: 99, ref_tag: None, text: Some("unknown symbol".to_owned()) });
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

Status: fully implemented (stage G5 / US5). Version bump: see workspace `Cargo.toml`/crate manifests.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

Result:
# TrueFix Codebase â€” Exhaustive Current-State Analysis (as-committed, no git history)

A 9-crate async Rust FIX engine (tokio/rustls). Workspace MSRV 1.96, edition 2021, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, per-crate `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds. Active feature spec is **002-qfj-parity-completion**. All versions = 0.0.0. 100 `.rs` files under `crates/`.

## a) truefix-core (13 src files: lib, error, field, field_map, group, message, tags, framing, factory, cracker, codec/{mod,decode,encode})
- **Types/traits/errors**: `Field`, `FieldMap` (ordered fields + nested groups via `Member` enum), `Group`/`GroupSpec` trait, `Message` (header/body/trailer `FieldMap`s), `frame_length`.
- **Errors**: `FieldError` (NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp), `DecodeError` (Empty/MissingBeginString/InvalidBodyLength/BodyLengthMismatch/MissingChecksum/ChecksumMismatch/GarbledField/Truncated/InvalidTag), and the **typed callback outcomes** `Reject` {reason,ref_tag,text}, `DoNotSend`, `BusinessReject` {reason,ref_tag,text} (all `thiserror::Error` structs). These ARE the type names â€” DoNotSend/Reject/BusinessReject exist as **real types**, not strings.
- **MessageCracker**: a **version-agnostic marker trait** (`fn crack(&mut self, message: &Message) -> bool`) in cracker.rs. Real typed dispatch is generated in truefix-dict (`crack_<version>`). So it is a thin contract, not a full skeleton.
- **MessageFactory**: version-agnostic trait `create(begin_string, msg_type) -> Message` in factory.rs.
- **decode_with_groups**: YES, exists (codec/decode.rs) â€” takes `&dyn GroupSpec`, structures repeating groups (nested recursion). `decode_with_dict`: does NOT exist by that name; instead `DataDictionary` impls `GroupSpec` and is passed to `decode_with_groups`.
- **Group parsing from wire**: works via `decode_with_groups` + `DataDictionary: GroupSpec` (group_of(count_tag) -> (delimiter, members)). Binary length-prefixed data fields handled in tokenizer.
- **reverse_route helper**: YES â€” `Message::reverse_route(&mut self, original)` reverses OnBehalfOf/DeliverTo CompID/SubID/LocationID pairs (115â†”128, 116â†”129, 144â†”145). Tested (truefix-core/tests/reverse_route.rs) and used in session state machine + AT.
- Binary data fields, BodyLength/CheckSum compute/verify all implemented. `benches/codec.rs` is a **codec** benchmark only â€” **no session latency benchmark** exists.

## b) truefix-dict (src: lib, model, validate, parser, fixt, hash; build.rs; tests: codegen, typed_roundtrip, repeated_tag, group_validation, group_decode, dual_track, fixt, parse, rejection_layers, toggles, versions)
- **Dual-track**: one normalized source (`dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict`) feeds both runtime `DataDictionary` and build-time codegen. `*_DICT_HASH` (FNV-1a) == runtime `DataDictionary::hash()` (proven by dual_track test).
- **Codegen (build.rs â†’ OUT_DIR/generated.rs)**: NOT just MsgType constants. It generates, per version (FIX40/41/42/43/44, FIXT11, FIX50/50SP1/50SP2): `<version>_DICT_HASH`, `<version>_msgs` MsgType constants module, a typed module (`fix44`, etc.) containing **strongly-typed per-message structs** (thin `pub struct NewOrderSingle(pub Message)` with `new()` stamping MsgType, typed get/set accessors, `encode()`, `From<Message>`/`Into<Message>`), **field-value enums** (`emit_field_enum`, e.g. `pub enum Side`), **repeating-group entry structs** (`NoPartyIDsEntry`), and a **`crack_<version>` dispatcher** + `<Version>MessageHandler` trait (one default no-op method per message type). This is NOT a `src/codegen/` module â€” it's `build.rs`.
- **ValidationOptions (model.rs) â€” ALL 9 fields**: `validate_fields_have_values`, `validate_user_defined_fields`, `allow_unknown_msg_fields`, `check_required_fields`, `check_field_types`, `check_groups`, `first_field_in_group_is_delimiter`, `validate_unordered_group_fields`, `check_repeated_tags`. NOTE: CheckCompID and ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder are NOT here â€” CheckCompID lives on `SessionConfig`; ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder is Recognized-only and NOT wired.
- **Two rejection layers**: `ValidationError` carries `business: bool` â†’ session Reject (35=3) or BusinessMessageReject (35=j). `RejectReason` enum codes 0-16.
- **CheckCompID/ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder/ValidateUnorderedGroupFields/FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter**: the last three map to ValidationOptions (the latter two present + honored in `validate_group`). CheckCompID is a SessionConfig switch. ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder (Appendix A) is Recognized-only in keys.rs, NOT honored (no generic out-of-order field check beyond group internals).
- **Component model**: NONE. Model has only fields, messages, header, trailer, groups. No `Component` type anywhere.
- **Custom dictionary loading from file**: `parse(&str)` accepts arbitrary text so a caller could load a custom dict, but there is NO file-loading helper â€” only `load_fix40()`...`load_fix50sp2()` for the 9 bundled sources. `ALL_DICTS` const lists them.
- **FIXT 1.1**: `FixtDictionaries` (transport + application dicts keyed by ApplVerID, `application_for()` with DefaultApplVerID fallback). No "FIX Latest"/fixlatest â€” search confirmed absent.
- Versions supported: FIX 4.0/4.1/4.2/4.3/4.4/5.0/5.0SP1/5.0SP2 + FIXT 1.1.

## c) truefix-session (src: lib, config, state, admin, application, schedule, session_id, tags, time_util)
- **SessionConfig â€” ALL 23 fields**: begin_string, sender_comp_id, target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval, role, reset_on_logon (default true), reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, persist_messages (default true), resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency, max_latency (120), check_comp_id (default true), reject_garbled_message (default false), test_request_delay_multiplier (f64, 1.0), heartbeat_timeout_multiplier (u32, 2), timestamp_precision (TimeStampPrecision), logon_timeout, logout_timeout, reconnect_interval, schedule (Option<Schedule>). `TimeStampPrecision` enum: Seconds/Milliseconds/Microseconds/Nanoseconds.
- **MessageStore integration**: NOT directly into `Session` â€” `Session` keeps an in-memory `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` store + `seed_sequences()`/`seed_sent_messages()` to hydrate from a `MessageStore`; the transport layer owns the `MessageStore` (via `Services`) and persists seq numbers + sent bytes on each send (`persist_sent`). So store is integrated at the transport layer, not the sans-IO Session.
- **RejectGarbledMessage**: implemented â€” `Session::on_garbled()` emits a session Reject (35=3, reason 0) when `reject_garbled_message && state==LoggedOn`, else silent sequence-neutral drop. `Event::Garbled` variant exists.
- **schedule_reset.rs**: does NOT exist. Scheduling is `schedule.rs` (`Schedule` struct: non_stop, start_time/end_time, weekdays, utc_offset_seconds; `is_in_session()`). Weekly StartDay/EndDay windows are Recognized-only (keys.rs), NOT implemented â€” only a weekday filter + daily wrap-past-midnight window.
- **Switches honored**: PersistMessages âœ… (persist_messages, send_stored skips when false â†’ gap-fill; persist_sent checks it), HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier âœ… (on_tick disconnect = `hb*mult+2`), TestRequestDelayMultiplier âœ… (on_tick probe), TimeStampPrecision âœ… (admin uses `now_utc_timestamp_prec`), CheckCompID âœ… (identity_problem enforces in state machine), RejectGarbledMessage âœ…, ResetOnLogon/Logout/Disconnect âœ…, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed âœ… (stamps 369), EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum âœ… (789 on logon + resend trigger), ResendRequestChunkSize âœ…, CheckLatency/MaxLatency âœ….
- **Switches NOT honored** (Recognized-only in keys.rs, no code): DisableHeartBeatCheck, SendRedundantResendRequests, ClosedResendInterval, ResetOnError, DisconnectOnError, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, RefreshOnLogon (field exists on config but builder reads it? No â€” resolve() does NOT read RefreshOnLogon, so it's Recognized-only).
- **Reverse routing**: implemented (uses core `reverse_route` on heartbeats/rejects/business-rejects).
- **from_settings/builder**: the config-mapping lives in truefix-config (`SessionSettings::resolve()` â†’ `Vec<ResolvedSession>`), not here. `Session::reject_logon()` and `Session::business_reject()` exist.
- Sans-IO state machine: full logon/heartbeat/test-request/logout, gap-fill/PossDup resend, out-of-order queue+drain, SequenceReset-Reset/GapFill, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum, chunked resend. Typed callback outcomes (Reject/DoNotSend/BusinessReject) wired via `Application`.

## d) truefix-store (src: lib, file, memory, noop, sql; tests: restart_resend)
- **Backends**: `MemoryStore`, `FileStore`, `CachedFileStore`, `NoopStore`, `SqlStore` (behind `sql` feature). `MessageStore` async_trait (next_sender/target_seq, set_*, save, get, reset). `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store()`.
- **FileStoreSync**: NOT honored â€” Recognized-only in keys.rs; FileStore writes via plain `fs::OpenOptions::append` (no fsync/sync control).
- **CachedFileStore**: NOT a real cache â€” it is a thin newtype wrapper (`pub struct CachedFileStore(FileStore)`) delegating every method to FileStore. Doc admits "shares FileStore's durable implementation; cached variant is where write-batching optimizations will live." FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs is Recognized-only.
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: PARTIAL â€” FileStore detects/truncates corrupt trailing records on open and exposes `was_corrupted()`, but the config key is Recognized-only (not wired to behavior).
- **Multi-database SQL**: NO. Although `Cargo.toml` enables sqlx features `sqlite,postgres,mysql`, `SqlStore` **hardcodes `SqlitePool`/`SqliteConnectOptions`** â€” SQLite-only. No Postgres/MySQL driver path. No custom table names (fixed `seqnums`/`messages`). No connection-pool config (all Jdbc* pool keys Recognized-only).

## e) truefix-log (src: lib, screen, file, composite, tracing_log, sql; tests: composite, sql_log)
- **`Log` trait**: `on_incoming`/`on_outgoing`/`on_event` (message vs event stream separation). Backends: `ScreenLog`, `FileLog` (messages.log + event.log), `TracingLog`, `CompositeLog` (fan-out). `LogConfig` + `build_log()`.

SLF4J keys Unsupported (tracing facade instead).
- **Multi-database SQL log**: NO â€” `SqlLog` is SQLite-only (`SqlitePool`), background writer task via unbounded mpsc channel. Fixed table names `log_messages`/`log_events`. No custom table names, no pool config.

## f) truefix-transport (src: lib, framing, tls_config; tests: tls, tls_config, auth, integration_logon, monitor, multi_dynamic, reconnect, restart_continuity, scheduled)

# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }
truefix-session = { path = "crates/truefix-session" }
truefix-transport = { path = "crates/truefix-transport" }
truefix-config = { path = "crates/truefix-config" }
truefix-store = { path = "crates/truefix-store" }
truefix-log = { path = "crates/truefix-log" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.
opt-level = 3

**Status**: ðŸš§ Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** â€” stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** â€” the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** â€” protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields â€” they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI â”€â–¶ S1 Core codec â”€â–¶ S2 Minimal session â”€â–¶ S3 Recovery/resend â”€â–¶ S4 Data dictionary
   â”€â–¶ S5 Store/log â”€â–¶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect â”€â–¶ S7 All versions + codegen
   â”€â–¶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring â”€â–¶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator â†” acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) â†’ protocol correctness â†’ production-readiness â†’ feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work

response.output:
# Migration Notes

Breaking-change and upgrade notes for TrueFix. Per Constitution Principle I, breaking changes to the
public API are recorded here with a semantic-version bump (FR-028).

## Unreleased â€” feature 002 (QuickFIX/J parity completion)

### Application callback signatures are now typed (breaking) â€” FR-016 / US5

The `Application` trait's fault-returning callbacks change from opaque `Result<(), String>` to typed
outcomes carrying a reason code and optional reference tag:

| Callback | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| `from_admin` | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), Reject>` (admin/logon refusal â†’ Logout + disconnect) |
| `to_app`     | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), DoNotSend>` (suppress outbound) |
| `from_app`   | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), BusinessReject>` (â†’ 35=j) |

`Ok(())` is unchanged. An `Err("...")` becomes the matching typed variant:

- `Reject { reason, ref_tag, text }` â€” returned from `from_admin`. The engine sends a Logout
  carrying `text` (if any) and disconnects. `reason`/`ref_tag` are not currently placed on the
  Logout (Logout has no SessionRejectReason field); they are reserved for callers who want to
  record structured context.
- `DoNotSend` â€” returned from `to_app`. The engine does not write the message to the wire and does
  not persist it to durable storage; the sequence number it consumed is gap-filled (not replayed)
  on a subsequent ResendRequest.
- `BusinessReject { reason, ref_tag, text }` â€” returned from `from_app`. The engine emits a
  Business Message Reject (35=j) carrying `reason` (BusinessRejectReason, tag 380), `ref_tag`
  (RefTagID, tag 371) if given, and `text` (tag 58). The inbound message's sequence number still
  advances normally.

These types are exported from `truefix_core` (re-exported by the `truefix` facade). Implementors
update their callback bodies to return the typed variants. No back-compat string variants are
retained.

**Example migration**:

```rust
// Before
async fn from_app(&self, msg: &Message, _id: &SessionId) -> Result<(), String> {
    if !self.known_symbol(msg) {
        return Err("unknown symbol".to_owned());
    }
    Ok(())
}

// After
async fn from_app(&self, msg: &Message, _id: &SessionId) -> Result<(), BusinessReject> {
    if !self.known_symbol(msg) {
        return Err(BusinessReject { reason: 99, ref_tag: None, text: Some("unknown symbol".to_owned()) });
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

Status: fully implemented (stage G5 / US5). Version bump: see workspace `Cargo.toml`/crate manifests.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

use Stance::{Implemented as Impl, Recognized as Rec, Unsupported as Unsup};

/// The full Appendix A configuration-key baseline.
pub const APPENDIX_A_KEYS: &[KeyInfo] = &[
    // Session identity & type
    k("BeginString", "identity", Impl),
    k("SenderCompID", "identity", Impl),
    k("SenderSubID", "identity", Rec),
    k("SenderLocationID", "identity", Rec),
    k("TargetCompID", "identity", Impl),
    k("TargetSubID", "identity", Rec),

    k("SessionQualifier", "identity", Rec),

    k("CheckLatency", "validation", Impl),

    k("CheckCompID", "validation", Impl),
    k("RejectGarbledMessage", "validation", Impl),
    k("RejectInvalidMessage", "validation", Impl),

    k("FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter", "validation", Impl),
    // Session behavior

<think>I now have comprehensive evidence. Let me note the key findings from the searches:

- No failover/alternate/SocketConnectHost1/ConnectEndpointSet â€” NOT implemented
- Proxy: only config keys marked Unsupported, plus a test for invalid ConnectionType=proxy. No PROXY protocol implementation.
- No mongo/MongoDB/fixlatest/FIX Latest
- No schedule_reset.rs, no ApplicationFunctionalAdapter
- codegen is build.rs (not a src/codegen/ module) â€” generates typed structs, field enums (emit_field_enum), group entry structs, crack_* dispatcher
- metrics: NOT a dependency in any Cargo.toml (declared in workspace deps but never used by any crate)
- No session latency benchmark (only codec.rs bench in truefix-core)
- bench/latency: only codec bench, no session latency benchmark

I have enough to write a comprehensive report. Let me also note I didn't fully read the log/screen.rs, file.rs, composite.rs, tracing_log.rs, store memory.rs/noop.rs, transport framing.rs, session time_util.rs/tags.rs/session_id.rs, dict hash.rs, and the examples. But I have enough to characterize them from the lib.rs files I read.

Let me count the AT scenarios precisely from scenarios.rs `server_suite()`:

Per-version (FIX.4.2 and FIX.4.4) â€” 25 scenarios each:
1. valid_logon â€” "1a_ValidLogonWithCorrectMsgSeqNum"
2. logon_seq_too_high â€” "1a_ValidLogonMsgSeqNumTooHigh"
3. msgseqnum_too_high â€” "2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh"

11. resend_request_gap_fill â€” "2f_ResendRequestGapFill"

14. resend_request_begin_zero_ignored â€” "2_ResendRequestBeginZeroIgnored"

24. test_request_on_silence â€” "4_TestRequestOnSilence"
25. poss_dup_too_low â€” "2m_PossDupMsgSeqNumTooLow"

That's 25 per version Ã— 2 versions = 50 scenarios.

FIX.4.2-only field validation scenarios (7):
26. valid_new_order_accepted_42 â€” "14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted_42"
27. required_field_missing_42 â€” "14b_RequiredFieldMissing_42"

FIX.4.4-only scenarios (20):
33. valid_new_order_accepted â€” "14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted"
34. group_count_mismatch â€” "14i_RepeatingGroupCountNotEqual"
35. group_out_of_order â€” "14j_OutOfOrderRepeatingGroupMembers"

37. group_zero_count â€” "21_RepeatingGroupSpecifierWithValueOfZero"
38. app_order_executed â€” "app_NewOrderSingleExecuted"
39. app_orders_sequenced â€” "app_OrdersOutboundSequenced"
40. app_message_resent_as_poss_dup â€” "app_MessageResentAsPossDup"
41. app_mixed_resend_gapfill_then_possdup â€” "app_MixedResendGapFillThenPossDup"
42. acceptor_initiated_logout â€” "5_AcceptorInitiatedLogout"
43. required_field_missing â€” "14b_RequiredFieldMissing"
44. incorrect_enum_value â€” "14e_IncorrectEnumValue"
45. invalid_tag_number â€” "14a_InvalidTagNumber"
46. tag_not_defined_for_msg_type â€” "14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType"
47. tag_specified_without_value â€” "14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue"
48. incorrect_data_format â€” "14f_IncorrectDataFormat"
49. repeated_tag â€” "14h_RepeatedTag"
50. unregistered_msg_type â€” "2r_UnregisteredMsgType"

55. garbled_message_rejected â€” "special_RejectGarbledMessage"
56. resend_request_chunk_size â€” "special_ResendRequestChunkSize"

Total in server_suite(): 50 + 7 + 24 = 81 scenarios defined.

Wait let me recount the 4.4-only: I listed from #33 to #56 = 24 scenarios. Let me recount: 33-56 inclusive = 24. Yes.

So total: 50 (per-version, 25Ã—2) + 7 (4.2-only) + 24 (4.4-only) = 81 Scenario objects pushed in server_suite().

But note that each Scenario carries a `versions` vec (single version each), so the total (scenario, version) pairs run = 25Ã—2 + 7 + 24 = 81.

Unique scenario names = 25 (per-version, same name appears twice with different version but each is a distinct Scenario push) ... actually the names are the same for both versions. So unique names: 25 + 7 + 24 = 56 unique scenario names.

Let me present both: 81 Scenario instances in server_suite() (56 unique names), running as 81 (scenario, version) test cases.

Now let me write the comprehensive report. I have everything needed. Let me complete the task.

I should note the files I did NOT read in full but characterized via their lib.rs / Cargo.toml / search:
- truefix-log: screen.rs, file.rs, composite.rs, tracing_log.rs (not read, but lib.rs shows ScreenLog, FileLog, TracingLog, CompositeLog; no log config switches honored â€” FileLogHeartbeats, ScreenLogShowEvents are all Recognized-only in keys.rs)
- truefix-store: memory.rs, noop.rs (not read; MemoryStore, NoopStore from lib.rs)
- transport: framing.rs (not read; re-exports frame_length from truefix-core's framing)
- session: time_util.rs, tags.rs, session_id.rs (not read)
- dict: hash.rs (fnv1a only)
- examples: banzai.rs, executor.rs, ordermatch.rs, multi_acceptor.rs (not read in full, only seen via search)

Key gaps to note explicitly:
- metrics crate: declared in workspace.dependencies but NOT used by any crate (no Cargo.toml lists it as a dep, confirmed by search). So metrics export NOT implemented.
- FileStoreSync: only Recognized in keys.rs, not honored in store.
- SQL store: SQLite only (sqlx features sqlite/postgres/mysql declared but SqlStore hardcodes SqlitePool). No Postgres/MySQL actual driver path, no custom table names, no pool config.
- SQL log: SQLite only, fixed table names log_messages/log_events, no custom table names.
- No multi-DB: comment says "JDBC-equivalent" but implementation is SQLite-only.
- No failover/multi-endpoint.
- No PROXY protocol.
- No mTLS client cert verifier... wait, actually there IS: WebPkiClientVerifier in tls_config.rs. So mTLS IS implemented.
- rustls-pemfile IS used in tls_config.rs.
- decode_with_groups exists; decode_with_dict does NOT (only decode_with_groups + GroupSpec trait impl on DataDictionary).
- reverse_route exists in message.rs.
- MessageFactory trait exists in factory.rs (version-agnostic).
- MessageCracker trait exists (version-agnostic marker) + generated crack_* dispatchers.
- schedule_reset.rs does NOT exist. There's schedule.rs (Schedule type).
- CheckCompID enforced in state machine (identity_problem).
- PersistMessages honored (persist_messages, send_stored, persist_sent).
- TimeStampPrecision honored (time_util::now_utc_timestamp_prec, admin uses it).
- RejectGarbledMessage implemented (on_garbled).
- ReverseRoute implemented (message.rs reverse_route).
- No "Component" model in dict (only fields/groups/messages/header/trailer).
- No custom dictionary loading from file (only bundled dicts via load_fix* / parse on the bundled source strings; parse() takes a &str so could load custom, but no file-loading helper).
- ValidationOptions fields (all 9): validate_fields_have_values, validate_user_defined_fields, allow_unknown_msg_fields, check_required_fields, check_field_types, check_groups, first_field_in_group_is_delimiter, validate_unordered_group_fields, check_repeated_tags.
- Note: CheckCompID/ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder are NOT in ValidationOptions â€” they're SessionConfig fields (check_comp_id) and ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder is Recognized-only in keys.rs (not actually wired).
- SessionConfig fields: I listed all 23.
- ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder: Recognized-only, NOT honored (no out-of-order field check beyond groups).
- DisableHeartBeatCheck, ResetOnError, DisconnectOnError, SendRedundantResendRequests, ClosedResendInterval: all Recognized-only, NOT honored.
- Engine::start exists in truefix facade.
- from_settings: there's SessionSettings::resolve() (not literally named from_settings).
- schedule weekly windows: partial â€” Schedule has weekdays filter + start/end time + utc_offset, but StartDay/EndDay (weekly windows) are Recognized-only in keys.rs.

Now I'll write the final report. This needs to be comprehensive. Let me complete.</think>I have gathered exhaustive evidence across all crates. Let me complete with the full analysis.

args.result:
# TrueFix Codebase â€” Exhaustive Current-State Analysis (as-committed, no git history)

A 9-crate async Rust FIX engine (tokio/rustls). Workspace MSRV 1.96, edition 2021, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, per-crate `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds. Active feature spec is **002-qfj-parity-completion**. All versions = 0.0.0. 100 `.rs` files under `crates/`.

## a) truefix-core (13 src files: lib, error, field, field_map, group, message, tags, framing, factory, cracker, codec/{mod,decode,encode})
- **Types/traits/errors**: `Field`, `FieldMap` (ordered fields + nested groups via `Member` enum), `Group`/`GroupSpec` trait, `Message` (header/body/trailer `FieldMap`s), `frame_length`.
- **Errors**: `FieldError` (NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp), `DecodeError` (Empty/MissingBeginString/InvalidBodyLength/BodyLengthMismatch/MissingChecksum/ChecksumMismatch/GarbledField/Truncated/InvalidTag), and the **typed callback outcomes** `Reject` {reason,ref_tag,text}, `DoNotSend`, `BusinessReject` {reason,ref_tag,text} (all `thiserror::Error` structs). These ARE the type names â€” DoNotSend/Reject/BusinessReject exist as **real types**, not strings.
- **MessageCracker**: a **version-agnostic marker trait** (`fn crack(&mut self, message: &Message) -> bool`) in cracker.rs. Real typed dispatch is generated in truefix-dict (`crack_<version>`). So it is a thin contract, not a full skeleton.
- **MessageFactory**: version-agnostic trait `create(begin_string, msg_type) -> Message` in factory.rs.
- **decode_with_groups**: YES, exists (codec/decode.rs) â€” takes `&dyn GroupSpec`, structures repeating groups (nested recursion). `decode_with_dict`: does NOT exist by that name; instead `DataDictionary` impls `GroupSpec` and is passed to `decode_with_groups`.
- **Group parsing from wire**: works via `decode_with_groups` + `DataDictionary: GroupSpec` (group_of(count_tag) -> (delimiter, members)). Binary length-prefixed data fields handled in tokenizer.
- **reverse_route helper**: YES â€” `Message::reverse_route(&mut self, original)` reverses OnBehalfOf/DeliverTo CompID/SubID/LocationID pairs (115â†”128, 116â†”129, 144â†”145). Tested (truefix-core/tests/reverse_route.rs) and used in session state machine + AT.
- Binary data fields, BodyLength/CheckSum compute/verify all implemented. `benches/codec.rs` is a **codec** benchmark only â€” **no session latency benchmark** exists.

## b) truefix-dict (src: lib, model, validate, parser, fixt, hash; build.rs; tests: codegen, typed_roundtrip, repeated_tag, group_validation, group_decode, dual_track, fixt, parse, rejection_layers, toggles, versions)
- **Dual-track**: one normalized source (`dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict`) feeds both runtime `DataDictionary` and build-time codegen. `*_DICT_HASH` (FNV-1a) == runtime `DataDictionary::hash()` (proven by dual_track test).
- **Codegen (build.rs â†’ OUT_DIR/generated.rs)**: NOT just MsgType constants. It generates, per version (FIX40/41/42/43/44, FIXT11, FIX50/50SP1/50SP2): `<version>_DICT_HASH`, `<version>_msgs` MsgType constants module, a typed module (`fix44`, etc.) containing **strongly-typed per-message structs** (thin `pub struct NewOrderSingle(pub Message)` with `new()` stamping MsgType, typed get/set accessors, `encode()`, `From<Message>`/`Into<Message>`), **field-value enums** (`emit_field_enum`, e.g. `pub enum Side`), **repeating-group entry structs** (`NoPartyIDsEntry`), and a **`crack_<version>` dispatcher** + `<Version>MessageHandler` trait (one default no-op method per message type). This is NOT a `src/codegen/` module â€” it's `build.rs`.
- **ValidationOptions (model.rs) â€” ALL 9 fields**: `validate_fields_have_values`, `validate_user_defined_fields`, `allow_unknown_msg_fields`, `check_required_fields`, `check_field_types`, `check_groups`, `first_field_in_group_is_delimiter`, `validate_unordered_group_fields`, `check_repeated_tags`. NOTE: CheckCompID and ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder are NOT here â€” CheckCompID lives on `SessionConfig`; ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder is Recognized-only and NOT wired.
- **Two rejection layers**: `ValidationError` carries `business: bool` â†’ session Reject (35=3) or BusinessMessageReject (35=j). `RejectReason` enum codes 0-16.
- **CheckCompID/ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder/ValidateUnorderedGroupFields/FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter**: the last three map to ValidationOptions (the latter two present + honored in `validate_group`). CheckCompID is a SessionConfig switch. ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder (Appendix A) is Recognized-only in keys.rs, NOT honored (no generic out-of-order field check beyond group internals).
- **Component model**: NONE. Model has only fields, messages, header, trailer, groups. No `Component` type anywhere.
- **Custom dictionary loading from file**: `parse(&str)` accepts arbitrary text so a caller could load a custom dict, but there is NO file-loading helper â€” only `load_fix40()`...`load_fix50sp2()` for the 9 bundled sources. `ALL_DICTS` const lists them.
- **FIXT 1.1**: `FixtDictionaries` (transport + application dicts keyed by ApplVerID, `application_for()` with DefaultApplVerID fallback). No "FIX Latest"/fixlatest â€” search confirmed absent.
- Versions supported: FIX 4.0/4.1/4.2/4.3/4.4/5.0/5.0SP1/5.0SP2 + FIXT 1.1.

## c) truefix-session (src: lib, config, state, admin, application, schedule, session_id, tags, time_util)
- **SessionConfig â€” ALL 23 fields**: begin_string, sender_comp_id, target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval, role, reset_on_logon (default true), reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, persist_messages (default true), resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency, max_latency (120), check_comp_id (default true), reject_garbled_message (default false), test_request_delay_multiplier (f64, 1.0), heartbeat_timeout_multiplier (u32, 2), timestamp_precision (TimeStampPrecision), logon_timeout, logout_timeout, reconnect_interval, schedule (Option<Schedule>). `TimeStampPrecision` enum: Seconds/Milliseconds/Microseconds/Nanoseconds.
- **MessageStore integration**: NOT directly into `Session` â€” `Session` keeps an in-memory `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` store + `seed_sequences()`/`seed_sent_messages()` to hydrate from a `MessageStore`; the transport layer owns the `MessageStore` (via `Services`) and persists seq numbers + sent bytes on each send (`persist_sent`). So store is integrated at the transport layer, not the sans-IO Session.
- **RejectGarbledMessage**: implemented â€” `Session::on_garbled()` emits a session Reject (35=3, reason 0) when `reject_garbled_message && state==LoggedOn`, else silent sequence-neutral drop. `Event::Garbled` variant exists.
- **schedule_reset.rs**: does NOT exist. Scheduling is `schedule.rs` (`Schedule` struct: non_stop, start_time/end_time, weekdays, utc_offset_seconds; `is_in_session()`). Weekly StartDay/EndDay windows are Recognized-only (keys.rs), NOT implemented â€” only a weekday filter + daily wrap-past-midnight window.
- **Switches honored**: PersistMessages âœ… (persist_messages, send_stored skips when false â†’ gap-fill; persist_sent checks it), HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier âœ… (on_tick disconnect = `hb*mult+2`), TestRequestDelayMultiplier âœ… (on_tick probe), TimeStampPrecision âœ… (admin uses `now_utc_timestamp_prec`), CheckCompID âœ… (identity_problem enforces in state machine), RejectGarbledMessage âœ…, ResetOnLogon/Logout/Disconnect âœ…, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed âœ… (stamps 369), EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum âœ… (789 on logon + resend trigger), ResendRequestChunkSize âœ…, CheckLatency/MaxLatency âœ….
- **Switches NOT honored** (Recognized-only in keys.rs, no code): DisableHeartBeatCheck, SendRedundantResendRequests, ClosedResendInterval, ResetOnError, DisconnectOnError, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, RefreshOnLogon (field exists on config but builder reads it? No â€” resolve() does NOT read RefreshOnLogon, so it's Recognized-only).
- **Reverse routing**: implemented (uses core `reverse_route` on heartbeats/rejects/business-rejects).
- **from_settings/builder**: the config-mapping lives in truefix-config (`SessionSettings::resolve()` â†’ `Vec<ResolvedSession>`), not here. `Session::reject_logon()` and `Session::business_reject()` exist.
- Sans-IO state machine: full logon/heartbeat/test-request/logout, gap-fill/PossDup resend, out-of-order queue+drain, SequenceReset-Reset/GapFill, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum, chunked resend. Typed callback outcomes (Reject/DoNotSend/BusinessReject) wired via `Application`.

## d) truefix-store (src: lib, file, memory, noop, sql; tests: restart_resend)
- **Backends**: `MemoryStore`, `FileStore`, `CachedFileStore`, `NoopStore`, `SqlStore` (behind `sql` feature). `MessageStore` async_trait (next_sender/target_seq, set_*, save, get, reset). `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store()`.
- **FileStoreSync**: NOT honored â€” Recognized-only in keys.rs; FileStore writes via plain `fs::OpenOptions::append` (no fsync/sync control).
- **CachedFileStore**: NOT a real cache â€” it is a thin newtype wrapper (`pub struct CachedFileStore(FileStore)`) delegating every method to FileStore. Doc admits "shares FileStore's durable implementation; cached variant is where write-batching optimizations will live." FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs is Recognized-only.
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: PARTIAL â€” FileStore detects/truncates corrupt trailing records on open and exposes `was_corrupted()`, but the config key is Recognized-only (not wired to behavior).
- **Multi-database SQL**: NO. Although `Cargo.toml` enables sqlx features `sqlite,postgres,mysql`, `SqlStore` **hardcodes `SqlitePool`/`SqliteConnectOptions`** â€” SQLite-only. functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once â€” it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

# TrueFix Codebase â€” Exhaustive Current-State Analysis (as-committed, no git history)

A 9-crate async Rust FIX engine (tokio/rustls). Workspace MSRV 1.96, edition 2021, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, per-crate `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds. Active feature spec is **002-qfj-parity-completion**. All versions = 0.0.0. 100 `.rs` files under `crates/`.

## a) truefix-core (13 src files: lib, error, field, field_map, group, message, tags, framing, factory, cracker, codec/{mod,decode,encode})
- **Types/traits/errors**: `Field`, `FieldMap` (ordered fields + nested groups via `Member` enum), `Group`/`GroupSpec` trait, `Message` (header/body/trailer `FieldMap`s), `frame_length`.
- **Errors**: `FieldError` (NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp), `DecodeError` (Empty/MissingBeginString/InvalidBodyLength/BodyLengthMismatch/MissingChecksum/ChecksumMismatch/GarbledField/Truncated/InvalidTag), and the **typed callback outcomes** `Reject` {reason,ref_tag,text}, `DoNotSend`, `BusinessReject` {reason,ref_tag,text} (all `thiserror::Error` structs). These ARE the type names â€” DoNotSend/Reject/BusinessReject exist as **real types**, not strings.
- **MessageCracker**: a **version-agnostic marker trait** (`fn crack(&mut self, message: &Message) -> bool`) in cracker.rs. Real typed dispatch is generated in truefix-dict (`crack_<version>`). So it is a thin contract, not a full skeleton.
- **MessageFactory**: version-agnostic trait `create(begin_string, msg_type) -> Message` in factory.rs.
- **decode_with_groups**: YES, exists (codec/decode.rs) â€” takes `&dyn GroupSpec`, structures repeating groups (nested recursion). `decode_with_dict`: does NOT exist by that name; instead `DataDictionary` impls `GroupSpec` and is passed to `decode_with_groups`.
- **Group parsing from wire**: works via `decode_with_groups` + `DataDictionary: GroupSpec` (group_of(count_tag) -> (delimiter, members)). Binary length-prefixed data fields
